source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT EDIA

Our mission is for every child to have an exceptional experience in school.



Edia is a Series A technology startup revolutionizing K-12 education with AI-driven solutions. We develop software to improve math education, tackle chronic absenteeism, and improve intervention processes for school districts. Today, Edia supports 200+ districts across the country (e.g. New York City, Denver, Fulton County, San Francisco, etc.).


ABOUT THE ROLE

Our customers hold us to a high bar, and it's our responsibility to create powerful, intuitive solutions that feel cohesive. The quality of your product decisions will have a direct impact on the confidence that school administrators have in us to solve their most important needs, especially as we utilize AI in completely new ways to rethink their workflows.

As a product manager at Edia, you'll have complete ownership to scope, define, and ship products 0 to 1. You'll be the CEO of your product—responsible not just for defining what to build, but for driving it to success. The ideal candidate is extremely smart, high-agency, has great taste, and is excited to shape how millions of students across the country experience school.



What You'll Do

 - Work with the CEO to shape an insanely compelling product vision

 - Meet with 100+ prospective clients to deeply understand their problems and validate solutions

 - Define product requirements, prioritize ruthlessly, and drive alignment across teams

 - Build functional prototypes and front-ends yourself using AI coding tools—you should be able to vibe code your way to a working product

 - Refine products with great attention to detail

 - Work with marketing to communicate that vision and captivate customers

 - Work tightly with engineering to ship high-quality products on time

 - Use data and customer feedback to iterate and expand key workflows

 - Define success metrics and hold yourself accountable to outcomes



Qualifications

 - You've built something great, ideally from scratch

 - 5+ years of product management / product design experience in software

 - Ability to build your own front-ends (or get pretty far) using tools like Claude Code, Cursor, or Replit

 - Extremely smart, high-agency, and relentlessly resourceful

 - Great taste—you know what good looks and feels like

 - You're fast and productive—you get thing done

 - Excellent written and verbal communication

 - Deep care about improving outcomes for students and educators

Work Authorization: We are currently unable to sponsor or transfer work authorization for all jobs. If things change in the future, we'll be sure to update this section.



We appreciate your interest in Edia. Feel free to follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/edia-app/ to learn more about what we're doing to improve education outcomes in the US.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, East
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT EDIA

Our mission is for every child to have an exceptional experience in school.



Edia is a Series A technology startup revolutionizing K-12 education with AI-driven solutions. We develop software to improve math education, tackle chronic absenteeism, and improve intervention processes for school districts. Today, Edia supports 200+ districts across the country (e.g. New York City, Denver, Fulton County, San Francisco, etc.).


ABOUT THE ROLE

At Edia, we believe every student deserves an exceptional experience at school. As an Enterprise Account Executive, your mission is not only to hit revenue goals but also to help districts unlock real outcomes for students. In this role, you’ll be shaping the trajectory of a high-growth company while directly influencing how schools across the country deliver education.



At full ramp, we expect our Enterprise AEs to achieve $250K in closed-won revenue per quarter while generating $800K in new pipeline, with a primary focus on net new logo acquisition across both net new and existing customer accounts. You’ll successfully manage 100 target accounts by driving high activity and strategic outreach efforts, resulting in 2 net new meetings per week and 5 total meetings per week. All while, maintaining a clean and accurate pipeline of 10-20 active opportunities per quarter in a high-growth, fast-paced environment.



At this time, we are prioritizing candidates who are located in one of the states listed in the job posting given territory alignment. Please note this is a field sales role and travel up to 50% of the time is required.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Revenue Generation

 - Close $250K in revenue per quarter (quarterly quota) through new and existing customers.

 - Build and maintain a healthy pipeline of at least $900K per quarter, ensuring consistent opportunity flow.



Strategic Prospecting & Outreach

 - Skilled at targeting key decision-makers within accounts and using personalized outreach strategies to open new opportunities.

 - Experience with multi-threaded account penetration and navigating complex B2B sales cycles.



Meeting Goals

 - Run at least 5 customer meetings per week, 2 of which need to be initial discovery meetings with new prospects or new contacts within existing accounts.



Pipeline Management

 - Maintain a clean and accurate pipeline in Salesforce/Clari across (on average) 10-20 qualified opportunities at any given time.

 - Ensure that pipeline stages, next steps, and close dates are consistently updated and reflective of the deal's actual status.



Adaptability & Creativity in a High-Growth Environment

 - Demonstrate the ability to operate effectively within a fast-growing, high-ambiguity segment of the business, using creative problem-solving and a proactive approach to uncover and address customer pain points.




QUALIFICATIONS:

Sales Execution

 - Proven ability to hit and exceed sales quotas in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with a minimum of 5+ years of Enterprise full cycle sales experience.

 - Expertise in full-cycle sales, from prospecting to closing, with a track record of driving $300K+ in quarterly revenue.



Strategic Prospecting & Outreach

 - Skilled at targeting key decision-makers within accounts and using personalized outreach strategies to open new opportunities.

 - Experience with multi-threaded account penetration and navigating complex B2B sales cycles.



Pipeline & Opportunity Management

 - Ability to manage a pipeline of 10-20 opportunities simultaneously, ensuring accuracy and cleanliness in Salesforce/Clari.

 - Proficiency in forecasting and managing deal progression to maintain a high-velocity sales process.



Communication & Relationship Building

 - Strong ability to build relationships and trust with key stakeholders, from managers to C-level executives.

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills, particularly in conveying complex software solutions.



Adaptability & Problem Solving

 - Comf

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT EDIA

Our mission is for every child to have an exceptional experience in school.



Edia is a Series A technology startup revolutionizing K-12 education with AI-driven solutions. We develop software to improve math education, tackle chronic absenteeism, and improve intervention processes for school districts. Today, Edia supports 200+ districts across the country (e.g. New York City, Denver, Fulton County, San Francisco, etc.).

About the Role
We're seeking a Senior Backend Engineer to join our team and help scale our Node.js applications on AWS. This role is different from a traditional engineering position: we've fully embraced AI-assisted development, and we expect most code to be written by AI tools. Your job is to direct that process effectively.


You'll work across our entire product suite and internal tools, building robust systems that serve educators and students while also using AI to automate our business operations.
What You'll Do

 - Architect and oversee backend systems across our product lineup and internal tooling

 - Guide AI coding assistants to implement large-scale changes and new features

 - Review, debug, and refine AI-generated code to production standards

 - Use AI internally to automate business operations

 - Ship full-stack features end-to-end



What We're Looking For


Essential skills:

 - Fast at reading code, human or AI generated

 - Strong debugging skills and an eye for bugs

 - Capacity to envision large architectural changes and break them into steps an AI can execute

 - Ability to context switch and juggle multiple in flight tasks

 - Experience with Node.js and cloud infrastructure (AWS, containers, managed databases)

 - Comfort working across multiple codebases and problem domains

What sets you apart:

 - You think at systems scale

 - You're effective at communicating intent to AI tools and iterating on their output

 - You have good judgment about when to accept AI suggestions and when to push back

 - You're comfortable with ambiguity and can take ownership of loosely-defined problems

Nice to have:

 - Frontend experience (React)—you'll be reading it regardless

 - Startup experience



Why This Role Is Different
We're not looking for someone who wants to write every line of code themselves. We're looking for someone who can leverage AI to move faster than traditional development allows, while maintaining the quality and reliability our school district partners depend on. If you're excited about working at the frontier of how software gets built, we'd love to hear from you.

Work Authorization: We are currently unable to sponsor or transfer work authorization for all jobs. If things change in the future, we'll be sure to update this section.



We appreciate your interest in Edia. Feel free to follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/edia-app/ to learn more about what we're doing to improve education outcomes in the US.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, SM Segment
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT EDIA

Our mission is for every child to have an exceptional experience in school.



Edia is a Series A technology startup revolutionizing K-12 education with AI-driven solutions. We develop software to improve math education, tackle chronic absenteeism, and improve intervention processes for school districts. Today, Edia supports 200+ districts across the country (e.g. New York City, Denver, Fulton County, San Francisco, etc.).


ABOUT THE ROLE

At Edia, we believe every student deserves an exceptional experience at school. As an Enterprise Account Executive, your mission is not only to hit revenue goals but also to help districts unlock real outcomes for students. In this role, you’ll be shaping the trajectory of a high-growth company while directly influencing how schools across the country deliver education.



At full ramp, we expect our AEs to achieve $125k in closed-won revenue per quarter while generating $400K in new pipeline, with a primary focus on net new logo acquisition across both net new and existing customer accounts. You’ll successfully manage 400 target accounts by driving high activity and strategic outreach efforts, resulting in 2 net new meetings per week and 5 total meetings per week. All while, maintaining a clean and accurate pipeline of 10-20 active opportunities per quarter in a high-growth, fast-paced environment.



At this time, we are prioritizing candidates who are located in one of the states listed in the job posting given territory alignment. Please note this is a field sales role and travel up to 50% of the time is required.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Close $500K / Year

 - Close $125k in revenue per quarter (quarterly quota) through new and existing customers, averaging 2-3 won deals per month.



Book 2+ Meetings / Week

 - Build and maintain a healthy pipeline of at least $500k per quarter, ensuring consistent opportunity flow.

 - Run at least 5 customer meetings per week, 2 of which need to be initial discovery meetings with new prospects or new contacts within existing accounts.
   
   

Salesforce Hygiene

 - Maintain a clean and accurate pipeline in Salesforce across (on average) 10-20 qualified opportunities at any given time.

 - Ensure that pipeline stages, next steps, and close dates are consistently updated and reflective of the deal's actual status.




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 2+ years of SMB full cycle sales experience.

 - Proven track record of hitting and exceeding sales quotas in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

 - Strong outbounding ability.

 - Extremely driven.



We also look for the following competencies and behaviors:

 - Grit – relentless drive to push through challenges and lead from the front.

 - Confidence – ability to make tough calls and stand behind them.

 - Curiosity – eagerness to learn from customers, team, and peers.

 - Intelligence – sharp commercial acumen and ability to simplify complexity.

Work Authorization: We are currently unable to sponsor or transfer work authorization for all jobs. If things change in the future, we'll be sure to update this section.



We appreciate your interest in Edia. Feel free to follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/edia-app/ to learn more about what we're doing to improve education outcomes in the US.

notes: Edia is a Series A technology startup in K-12 education with AI-driven solutions.
